1.description: (Color online) The Composition of Lending around Banking Crises Notes. This figure shows the dollar amount of loan amounts arranged (panel (a)) or committed (panel (b)) by foreign lenders and low market share lenders out of the dollar amount of all loans committed or arranged in a country in a year for the periods leading to banking crises and in normal times. Our sample includes 64 banking crises in 46 affected countries during the 1986–2016 period. The banking crises are categorized as in Baron et al. (2021) . The y -axis represents the average percentage of loan amount arranged or retained by foreign lenders across banking crises in the left panel and that by low market share lenders in the right panel. For each precrisis and crisis year from t = −5 to t + 1, we compute the proportion of the loan amounts arranged or committed by foreign lenders and low market share lenders. Low market share lenders are defined as lenders whose five-year lagged market retained share is less than 1%. The solid lines plot the loan composition during the precrisis period, whereas the dotted lines plot the loan composition during normal times as a benchmark. For each crisis country, we select as normal times six consecutive year periods outside the four precrisis years, crisis year, and one year after the crisis. The x -axis represents the year relative to banking crisis from t = − 5 to t = 1. (a) Arranged amount. (b) Retained amount.
